heaviest_rainfall.status: The most intense rainfall recorded took place on 09 November 2025, with precipitation totalling 47.89mm.  The rain fell at 24.6mm per hour at times over the course of the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: A torrential downpour occurred on 02 September 2025 when the rain reached a maximum intensity of 34.3mm per hour.  This happened during a 15.0 hour storm when 21.19mm of rain fell.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning activity recorded in a single day is 225, which occurred on 28 May 2026 (since our records began on 11 April 2025).

storm.status: No stormy weather is anticipated.  The last big storm was recorded on 18 July 2026, lasting 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: A significant dry period is on-going and is ranked 1st for length since 23 September 2024.  So far, it has lasted 20 consecutive days beginning 03 July 2026.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is currently no cold snap.  This year there have been 3 cold snaps, usually there are 3.7 cold snaps per year.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 27.7°C from 24 April 2026 (when the temperature reached 23.2°C but fell to -4.5°C).

heatwaves.status: A heatwave is active and has been ongoing for 22 days since 2026-07-01.  This year has seen 3 heatwaves, compared to an average of 1.9 per year.

tropical.status: The most recent tropical night was on 28 June 2026 and this has only occurred 68 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (211°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: 128.2 mph is the largest wind gust recorded on 27 January 2026.

run.status: The largest wind run ever was on 21 October 2012 with a value of 1737 miles.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 3.2°C) and outside temperature (down 2.6°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 15%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 13282 lux) and cloud levels (up 1615 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 15007 lux) and cloud levels (up 1828 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 1614.8m lower than yesterday, 1827.6m lower than the past week, 1148.9m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 1829m to a maximum of 6260m, averaging 2610m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 4440m and 3760m respectively.
cloud levels.summary: The clouds will be high, plenty of sunshine is expected.

summary.summary: Inside; hot temperatures are forecast. Outside; hot temperatures will prevail. Also, a strong muggy feeling will dominate and near calm conditions. Chance of light rain, the clouds will be high, plenty of sunshine is expected, and sunlight levels will peak at very high intensity. Similar weather conditions should remain.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
